no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what needs to be implemented (trait methods, helper functions, watermark format) and the requirements, but does not give exact code or line-by-line changes. The developer still needs to reason about the implementation details.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests test_watermark_path_helper_exists, test_get_watermark_helpers_exist, and test_object_store_watermark_struct_exists use string matching like 'async fn get_watermark_for_read', 'async fn get_watermark_for_write', 'fn watermark_path', 'reader_lo: u64', 'pruner_hi: u64', 'pruner_timestamp_ms: u64'. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests test_watermark_path_helper_exists, test_object_store_watermark_struct_exists, and test_get_watermark_helpers_exist are purely text-based checks (assert 'fn watermark_path' in content, assert 'reader_lo: u64' in content, etc.) with no execution. While the behavioral tests do execute cargo test subprocesses, these three structural tests are purely textual.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests run actual cargo tests that exercise the ConcurrentConnection trait methods. The check_test_actually_ran function verifies tests were found and ran (not just that cargo exited 0). The stub implementations (returning Ok(None) or bailing) would cause the Rust unit tests to fail their assertions on returned values.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution/solve.sh downloads the fixed file from a specific GitHub URL (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MystenLabs/sui/92cdfc7fe041bf824567e76995a5d5ef973bc6d6/crates/sui-indexer-alt-object-store/src/lib.rs). An agent could read /workspace/sui's solve.sh or solution directory to find this URL and simply curl the answer. The Dockerfile doesn't remove or protect the solution directory.
